---
title: "Big Floatingheart (Nymphoides aquatica)"
slug: "nymphoides-aquatica"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474992715/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Big Floatingheart"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Asterales"
tax_family: "Menyanthaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Asterales", "Menyanthaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474992715/medium.jpg" alt="Big Floatingheart" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/swagner23">
swagner23</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264523031">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Asterales
-   **Family:** Menyanthaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=165757)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in still or slow-moving freshwater habitats.

## Natural history notes

An aquatic plant characterized by its round, floating leaves often marked with reddish patterns and its showy white flowers with fringed petals. In Florida, it is found in still or slow-moving freshwater habitats such as ponds, lakes, and marshes. It grows during the warmer months and provides cover and habitat for aquatic organisms.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/165757-Nymphoides-aquatica)
-   [Local profile](https://www.wildflower.org/plants/result.php?id_plant=NYAQ)
